mechanism_narrative: A persistently low and stable federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and reduces the attractiveness of cash substitutes, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as FEDFUNDS remains near cycle lows.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or returns during low-rate periods are not statistically distinguishable from full-sample baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades and risk-off equity flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the deterioration signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) jointly confirms a self-reinforcing consumer-driven expansion that is not detectable from either series alone, and this joint confirmation predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND PAYEMS 3-month change positive
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances fail to outperform single-series baseline signals by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the joint signal is not higher than the max of the individual series' IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery regime, rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects reaccelerating manufacturing output and capacity utilization that feeds into corporate earnings growth, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: INDPRO trend shows no significant correlation (Rank IC < 0.03) with 60-day forward returns during the recovery regime over any 2-year rolling window, or effect reverses sign outside recovery periods without being flagged.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in CPIAUCSL above a threshold signals rising inflation pressure that raises expectations of future monetary tightening, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered CPI surprises are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) across at least two independent historical episodes, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in housing starts (HOUST) signals improving household confidence and construction-sector demand that spills over into broader consumer and investment spending, generating a positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST 3-month trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or HOUST uptrends coincide with negative average forward returns in more than one distinct historical period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while the unemployment rate is still elevated relative to pre-crisis norms, the implied policy gap signals an extended accommodative stance that markets price as durable support for risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; the effect requires the joint condition of low rates AND high unemployment rather than either alone.
regime_hint: zero-lower-bound accommodative monetary regime with slack labor market
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods show 60-day forward returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction signal's Rank IC does not exceed either single-series signal's IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of confirmed post-recession GDP growth (GDPC1 rising for at least two consecutive quarters), positive month-over-month retail sales growth (RSAFS) signals continuing near-term consumer momentum, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days; outside this recovery regime the relationship is expected to be weak or absent.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 positive for 2 consecutive quarters AND RSAFS month-over-month change > 0
failure_condition: During confirmed recovery regime, 5-day forward returns following positive RSAFS prints are not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows within the regime.
